SOURCE_LANGUAGE: Java
DOC FORMAT: Javadoc

SYNTAX
======
- Block starts with /** on its own line, every continuation line with
  ' * ' (space, asterisk, space), closes with ' */'.
- Indentation matches the method/class declaration exactly.
- First line: concise, active one-liner. NO 'This method ...'.
- First line ends with a period - Javadoc uses it as the short summary
  on overview pages.
- Blank line before the block tags.

BLOCK TAGS (in this order, only when applicable)
================================================
@param <n> <description ending with a period.>
@return <description ending with a period.>     (only when return type != void)
@throws <FullyQualifiedException> <reason.>     (both checked and unchecked)
@see <reference>                                 (e.g. {@link OtherClass})
@since <version>                                 (only if used by the project)
@deprecated <reason + migration hint.>           (when @Deprecated is present)

RULES
=====
- Inline tags for cross-references:
    {@link FullyQualified.Name}      - clickable reference
    {@code identifier}               - monospace without HTML escaping
    {@literal text}                  - literal text, special chars allowed
- HTML tags allowed in descriptions: <p>, <code>, <em>, <ul><li>. Use
  sparingly - prefer {@code ...} over <code>...</code>.
- For generic types: @param <T> Description of the type parameter.
- Document RuntimeExceptions with @throws when they are thrown
  intentionally (e.g. IllegalArgumentException for validation).
- @return is NEVER used for void methods or constructors.
- Always include @deprecated when the @Deprecated annotation is present.

EXAMPLE
=======
/**
 * Calculates the gross total including value-added tax.
 *
 * @param items   List of line items to total.
 * @param taxRate Tax rate as a decimal (e.g. {@code 0.19} for 19 %).
 * @return Gross total in the same currency as the line items.
 * @throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException When {@code items} is empty.
 */
